resources.string: Share a phone-connected USB device with a remote computer.

Plug devices into the phone via OTG. Every attached non-hub device is offered to the remote computer (it picks one with usbip attach -b <busid>). The first computer that connects with your invite becomes the owner.
USB device: none — plug one in via OTG
Waiting for a computer to connect with your invite…
Copy this and give it to the remote computer so it can reach you and become an owner.
The invite is the only secret. Anyone who knows it can attach. Reset to rotate the keypair: the current invite stops working and a fresh one is generated.
The current invite will stop working immediately. Any remote computer that still has it will need the new invite to connect.
Identity rotated — copy the new invite from the main screen
Could not rotate identity — see logcat tag wsusb
Known hosts auto-bind the matching relay key. Default is wss://tele.karlson.ru/ws. Change requires Stop sharing → start to take effect.
URL must start with ws:// or wss://
Custom host — relay keys will use the built-in fallback. Edit only if you know the relay\'s X25519/Ed25519 public keys match.

My name is Leo Kaganov. I'm an engineer working on embedded devices and network communication protocols. If you like my work and your company has tasks for me, I'd be glad to collaborate:

LLeo@LLeo.me
Telegram: @lleokaganov

wsusb shares a USB device plugged into this phone with a remote computer through an encrypted relay, so the computer sees the device as if it were locally attached.\n\nThe phone is the gate. The computer becomes an owner on first connection (TOFU).
For the relay to stay connected while the screen is off, please remove battery restrictions for wsusb in the next screen.
